Ulyana Piterbarg

Hey there! I’m a rising senior studying course 18C at MIT.

About Me

I am broadly interested in representation learning, Bayesian inference, and the design of optimal decision-making schemes for environments with complex dynamics.

Outside of academics, I represent undergraduates in the MIT math department via the Committee for Math Majors (CoMM).

Research

I am currently an undergraduate research assistant in Josh Tenenbaum’s lab, studying (1) the cognitive bases of strategy learning, (2) hierarchical reinforcement learning, and (3) deep learning algorithms for fluid manipulation tasks.

This coming fall, I will be joining the Climate Modeling Alliance to work with Raffaele Ferrari on leveraging inference and machine learning methods to develop novel parameterizations of turbulence and stochastic dynamics in large-scale planetary flows as an MIT Quest for Intelligence Advanced Undergraduate Research Scholar.

Publications

  1. *KR.Allen, *KA.Smith, U.Piterbarg, R.Chen, JB.Tenenbaum: Abstract strategy learning underlies flexible transfer in physical problem solving. CogSci 2020.
